name: Rex Stewart
abstract: Rex William Stewart Jr. (February 22, 1907 – September 7, 1967) was an American jazz cornetist who was a member of the Duke Ellington orchestra.

works.t: Black, Brown & Beige: Lighter Attitude (Emancipation Proclamation)
Black, Brown & Beige: West Indian Influence
Black, Brown and Beige: Three Dances

concerts.label: Bel Canto Foundation Jazz Concert to Benefit Musicians
Midnight Performance: Concert of Jazz Dance Theatre

about_quotes.speaker: Doc Cheatham
about_quotes.evidence: my whole life I had a list of the trumpet players that I've played with, it would take maybe three or four hours to name the trumpet players I've played with. From Joe Smith, to Rex Stewart

about_quotes.speaker: Hentoff Nat
about_quotes.evidence: with some of them, like Rex Stewart, for example, we became quite good friends. We socialized together when he was in town, at mutual friends’ homes.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hentoff Nat — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Carter Benny
about_quotes.evidence: I had one gig, an afternoon gig that Rex Stewart took me on, too, because they were lacking . . . something had happened to their saxophonist.
about_quotes.doc_title: Carter Benny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Pierre Merlin
about_quotes.evidence: I was, of course, influenced by the records I heard. Rex Stewart and Biggs Bayder Beck and Armstrong, of course.

about_quotes.speaker: Ram Ramirez
about_quotes.evidence: I left the band... When I left the band I went to Rex Stewart. ... And Rex, of course.

about_quotes.speaker: Horace Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: I had the late and great Rex Stewart playing cornet with me in Wilberforce.
